entry.title: Bloquear actualización del Address ID (CRD1) con Transaction Notification en SAP Business One HANA
entry.content:
Estoy trabajando con SAP Business One sobre HANA y necesito evitar que se actualice el campo Address(ID de dirección) en la tablaCRD1 , pero permitiendo

La creación de nuevas direcciones.

La actualizacion de otros campos del Socio de Negocio

El control lo estoy implementando mediante SBO_SP_TransactionNotification, validando el objeto Business Partner (OCRD) .

El problema es que, al intentar validar la modificación del ID de dirección, las condiciones terminan bloqueando otros cambios del socio de negocio o la creación.

¿Cuál es la mejor forma de detectar únicamente la actualización del ID de dirección en CRD1 dentro de Transaction Notification en HANA, sin afectar las demás operaciones?

Agradecería un ejemplo o recomendación de lógica para este escenario.

entry.title: Re: How can I allow a communication user to schedule a ba...
entry.content:
Hi Sandra,

Thanks for the detailed reply. I guess it's the wrong question to be asking.

The requirement is to allow a specific set of asynchronous work to occur as a reaction to a user's interaction. The user has the authorization to raise the event that triggers the asynchronous work to occur, but must not have the authorizations that are needed to perform asynchronous work itself.

We already have a roundtrip framework using an ABAP RAP based OData API v4 service that gets the processing of the asynchronous work to a Communication User with the appropriate authorizations to perform the tasks. The challenge is that the context is now in an ABAP RAP action execution. The asynchronous workload can be quite high, and it requires multiple commits during processing. This is not possible in the context of a RAP action execution. The bgPF framework could certainly be used to split up the work and I understand this work is done in a particular series, but all work packages need to report back to the overall execution to so it can calculate and set an overall result from all of the processing. An application job would work perfectly for this and it really irks me that this limitation exists. But I guess we'll need to keep searching for alternatives. Maybe hacking together some sort of DB persistence with a final bgPF 'finalize' processing step or something might work.

Thanks again.

entry.title: SAP Signavio Dictionaries
entry.content:
Hello,
I’m looking for some guidance on how you handle situations where swimlanes and dictionary entries are normally based on business roles, but certain tasks can be performed by anyone within a specific group or team, regardless of their formal position.
For example: Fire Wardens. This responsibility sits on top of a person’s nominal role, and it isn’t tied to a single area of the business.
Do you create a separate dictionary category for Responsibilities (e.g., Fire Warden), distinct from business roles?
If so:

How do you define and structure those responsibility entries?
And how does someone easily identify or contact the people assigned to that group when needed?

Thanks in advance for your insights.

entry.title: Re: Use SAP SuccessFactors Compensation to Provide Pay Equity Adjustments
entry.content:
@FL19  this set up is deliberate from my part as we are working on average salaries off the starting salaries.  If you wanted to use the modified salaries using this configuration, you would have look at doing a two step process where first all merit increases are applied and then the average male salary is calculated off of the modified salaries and loaded into the lookup table as appropriate.  I do think though for accurate reporting, you have to pick data as at a point in time either before or after all merit increases are applied. 

Keep in mind this example is simple and the blog was created to provide an example of what is possible using the Compensation module functionality.  It is very likely that real life examples will require more variables to determine the gender gap and both organisations and geographical regions will defer in their approaches to determine this.  Hope that helps.

entry.title: Fiori My Inbox: WITH_URL Annotation Always Shows URL Instead of Custom Text
entry.content:
Dear Community,

 

I am doing an enhancement in My Inbox app, where I want to add a url to a specific item row.  Here I am using two calculated fields, one to determine the text and another to determine the URL to be used. Now I have done the following in my CDS:

@Semantics.url: {

mimeType: 'url'

}

@ObjectModel.readOnly: true

@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_REVIEWINVOICE'

@ObjectModel.virtualElement: true

cast (' ' as abap.char(1024) )

as WEBSITEURL,

@ui.lineItem: [ {

position: 14,

importance: #HIGH,

type: #WITH_URL,

label : 'Webside URL' ,

value: 'REVIEWINVOICE' , //what users sees

url: 'WEBSITEURL' //actual link

} ]

@Semantics.url: {

mimeType: 'url'

}

@ObjectModel.readOnly: true

@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_REVIEWINVOICE'

@ObjectModel.virtualElement: true

cast( ' ' as abap.char(1024) ) as REVIEWINVOICE,

 

I am trying to display the virtual calculated text message REVIEWINVOICE and show the other virtual calculated URL WEBSITEURL when clicking. Problem is the value and the text seem to overwrite each other somehow. The URL loading seems to work only when i put WEBSITEURL in the value section.

Additionally if I comment the @Semantics.url for REVIEWINVOICE, I cannot click on the link anymore.

Any Ideas ideas what am I doing wrong here. I wanted to mention that in IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ~CALCULATE, I manage determine both the text REVIEWINVOICE and the URL for WEBSITEURL

Thanks and looking forward for any ideas on this topic.

entry.title: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content:
Hi folks.

Does anybody know a way in SAC to retrieve the value at a parent node in a Data Action? (or Calc Measure for that matter).

My specific use case is: I want to retrieve the (aggregated) value of a particular measure at the parent of a given member and write that value to another measure, across a set of multiple specific members.
What I wish I could do would simply be something like:
DATA(Measure=TargetMeasure) = RESULTLOOKUP(Measure=Amount, MyDim=MyDim.MyParent) but that isn't supported.

In KBA 279311, SAP notes the limitation that RESULTLOOKUP only supports leaf members, and states "The feature to Support Parent/Formula member in Resultlookup in Data Action is in our backlog for future consideration".
Well it's been around 7 years since that was issued and it still isn't available. It's proving unbelievably difficult to simply grab a value from a hierarchy parent.

That same KBA suggests manually recomputing by summing the leaf members to reconstruct the parent total, but how can we do that in a dynamic way? We can only really use BASEMEMBER in a MEMBERSET statement, and those can't be used in a dynamic fasion, e.g. within conditional logic or loops. Otherwise I would happily just loop over all my relevant members and redeclare MEMBERSET = BASEMEMBER(MyParent) for each one, but that doesn't work either.

Considering how central hierarchies are to how SAC works, it is mind-boggling that you cannot simply look up the value on a parent node in either Calculated Measures or Data Actions. I understand that the Query Engine and the Calc Engine work differently but come on!

The only way I can come up with so far is to maintain explicit rollup attributes for every individual level of the hierarchy against every leaf member and construct the values from the bottom up for all ancestor nodes, looping over all leaves but this seems frankly ridiculous to have to maintain for multiple hierarchies with multiple levels. 

If anybody knows a better way to approach this, I would be grateful to hear it.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content:
Hi @ebajema1 ,

There is a Global Configuration function available which can help you to achieve the desired result.

CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES = ON

Below example copies the value from Parent Node to a Base Member.

Before DA Execution:

After DA Execution

Data Action Script

CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES = ON
CONFIG.HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY = [d/M1_PRODUCT]
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YQM] ,"[2025]" )
DATA([d/Measures]="QUANTITY",[d/M1_PRODUCT]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/M1_PRODUCT]="P_TOT",[d/M1_GL_ACCOUNT]="Units Sold",[d/Measures]="QUANTITY")

Please refer to the below link for details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html?locale=en-US#config.read_calculated_member_values

Note from the link states the below current limitation 

  • This configuration function is only supported on SAP Analytics Cloud tenants based on SAP HANA Cloud.
  • Alternatively, you can achieve similar functional results by utilizing a data copy step within the data action to store calculation results before performing any additional dependent calculations or operations.

entry.title: SAP IBP - Profit Center Description Not Appearing in Planning Objects
entry.content:
Business Impact:
The Profit Center Description attribute is not appearing in planning objects or planning views, even though it is correctly maintained in Profit Center master data and is visible as a referenced attribute. This gap prevents planners from validating Profit Center‑level targets, aggregations, and reporting in the IBP Excel UI. As a result, several planning activities that depend on Profit Center–based dimensions cannot proceed.

Operational Consequence:
Planners cannot differentiate or validate data by Profit Center, which directly impacts:

  • Inventory & SLOB Target value planning
  • Profit Center–level aggregation and disaggregation logic
  • Planning template validation and KPI roll‑ups
  • Downstream reports and decision‑making dependent on Profit Center segmentation

Urgency:
The issue blocks functional testing for Profit Center‑related requirements and is delaying project milestones. Resolution is required urgently to avoid slippage in current UAT and deployment timelines.

Expected Outcome from SAP:
We need SAP to identify why referenced attributes from Profit Center MDT are not being populated into planning objects despite correct configuration and activation. We expect guidance on model consistency checks, attribute propagation, or any required corrective actions at the application layer.

Summary of the Issue Observed in SAP IBP

The screenshots show the Master Data Maintenance view for a particular Master Data Type where a Reference Attribute has been configured but is not appearing in the Planning Object view.

Key Observations

  1. Master Data Maintenance (MDM) UI shows the attribute correctly

    • The attribute is visible in the MDM screen as a standard master data attribute.
    • This confirms that the attribute exists in the master data type definition.

  2. But the same attribute does not appear in the Planning Object view

    • When navigating to the Planning Object for the same entity, the attribute is missing.
    • This indicates a mismatch between the master data definition and the Planning Object model.

  3. Screenshots highlight the same field across 3 GUI views

    • All three slides display three different UI sections where the attribute is expected.
    • Each screenshot highlights the area where the attribute should appear but doesn’t.

  4. Likely root cause (based on typical IBP behavior)
    Although not in the file itself, the situation strongly suggests one of these IBP configuration gaps:

    • The attribute is not marked as “key figure relevant” or not included in the Planning Level.
    • The attribute may be missing in the Master Data Type – Planning Level assignment.
    • A cold model activation or attribute propagation may not have been refreshed.
    • The attribute is a Reference Attribute, which requires explicit inclusion in the Planning Model structure before it becomes visible.

Short explanation:

“The Reference Attribute is visible in Master Data Maintenance but does not appear in the Planning Object overview. This suggests that the attribute exists in the master data definition but is not propagated to the Planning Model due to missing assignment in the Planning Level, attribute relevance settings, or incomplete model activation.”

Has anyone else had to do this migration of archived objects between content repositories previously.

Cheers Nathan

entry.title: Re: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content:
@ebajema1 

There is an option to enable reading of data on parent/calculated members using a configuration. This is only supported on SAC tenant on HANA Cloud.

CONFIG.READ_CALUCATED_MEMBER_VALUES = ON

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html#config.read_calculated_member_values

The only other option to aggregate in a dynamic way is to create an attribute for each hierarchy which you covered.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: CPE-UM Environment Activation Remains ‘Is Finished = No’ – How to Identify and Resolve the Issue
entry.content:
In the PaPM Cloud Universal Model, users frequently face an issue where the CPE–UM environment activation remains in the ‘Is Finished’ state as ‘No’ indefinitely, and no error logs are recorded for the Activate event.

In such cases, the environment activation does not end in either ‘Error’ or ‘Yes’ status. Instead, it remains ‘No’ forever after activation is triggered. In some scenarios, the environment activation ends with an error, but the error message itself is not meaningful. It does not clearly indicate the root cause or specify which function is causing the issue, making it difficult to identify configuration bugs.

In PaPM On-Premise, the system clearly highlights the function with errors using an error icon on the individual function. However, in the Universal Model, no such indication is available. Only the Runtime Status of a function is displayed as Current, Not Current, or Inactive, which is not sufficient to identify configuration issues or bugs.

Due to this limitation, users often end up raising an OSS ticket to resolve the issue.

However, this issue can often be resolved internally by using the Validate option available in the Manage Environment tile. The Validate option checks the entire environment configuration for inconsistencies and clearly reports (if any errors):

  1. Detailed error messages
  2. The affected Function ID(s)
  3. Relevant information in the event logs


For environments with a large number of functions and complex calculations, it is strongly recommended to validate the environment before activation. This helps identify configuration issues early and saves significant time and effort that would otherwise be spent troubleshooting activation failures.

Using the Validate option before activating the environment helps ensure configuration consistency and avoids unnecessary confusion during activation.

Conclusion
When a CPE–UM environment activation remains in Is Finished = No, it becomes difficult to identify the root cause due to limited error details. Using the Validate option in the Manage Environment tile helps detect configuration issues and provides clear error information before activation. Validating the environment in advance can save time and avoid unnecessary troubleshooting.

Thank you for reading this blog post.
If there are any questions or doubts, please feel free to leave a comment. I’ll be happy to help. Have a great day!

entry.title: Promotion of Variants from BO 4.2 to BI2025
entry.content:
When an SAP BusinessObjects system needs to be upgraded and the underlying architecture of the server is no longer supported, a migration project will be needed. This includes installing a new server and promoting all objects from the old to the new environment.

Most objects can then be transferred using the Promotion Job embedded in the Central Management Console (CMC). However, the Variants are not included in the Promotion Jobs via CMC when the source is a 4.2 environment, and you cannot select them anywhere.

Here is how to work around this limitation:

Prerequisite

  • All reports containing variants (WebI and Analysis Office) must already be promoted to the new environment.
  • The Analysis Office Property for Variants support must be set to "User and Global"

Step 1: Create an LCMBIAR file via LCM Command Line (LCMCLI)

As described in SAP Note 1634962, create a Properties file containing the necessary logon information and the query to be executed during LCMBIAR file creation.

The Properties file should contain the following:

exportLocation= [e.g. C:\LCMBIAR\]
lcmbiarpassword=
action=export
LCM_CMS=localhost:6400
LCM_userName=Administrator
LCM_password=password
LCM_authentication=secEnterprise
Source_CMS=localhost:6400
Source_userName=Administrator
Source_password=password
Source_authentication=secEnterprise
exportQuery1: [see queries below]

Query options:

Two different queries have to be used to export variants to an LCMBIAR file:

select top 10000 * from ci_appobjects where descendants("si_name='folder hierarchy'", "si_cuid='AVdHJc0LnWdJvVPozhNE.Ak'"):
select top 10000 * from ci_appobjects where si_kind = 'BIVariant'

The objective is to generate two separate LCMBIAR files for import into the new system. Create two properties files with different queries, or reuse a single file by modifying the query between executions. Since the LCMBIAR filename includes a timestamp, each execution produces a distinct file.

Execute the LCM Command Line tool

1. Open a command window and navigate to:  

<SAP BO installation>\SAP BusinessObjects\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64\scripts

2. Run the following command: 

lcm_cli.bat -lcmproperty <property file>

Step 2: Import the LCMBIAR file

Import the generated LCMBIAR files into the new environment via CMC > Promotion Management.

Importing the files links the variants to their respective reports. Verify this by running the following query in AdminTools:

select SI_ID, SI_CUID, SI_NAME, SI_BIVARIANTS_OF_DOCUMENT from ci_infoobjects where si_cuid = <CUID of a report containing variants>

The result set should display one or more entries for the SI_BIVARIANTS_OF_DOCUMENT property.

Step 3: Activate BIVariants authorization

Although variants are linked to reports (verifiable in AdminTools), not all variants will be visible when opening reports. Only global variants created by the current user appear by default. To make global variants created by other users visible, implement the solution from SAP Note 3283926.

  • Download the BIVariants.js file from SAP Note 3283926
  • Modify the Mode parameter to SET_RIGHTS_FOR_AO
  • Execute the script via Command Window (CMD) > cscript BIvariants.js

This enables visibility of all global variants across users.

Conclusion

By following these steps, you will successfully work around the Variant migration limitation in SAP BusinessObjects 4.2. It ensures that users do not have to recreated their customized report Variants from scratch in the new environment.

entry.title: Streamline User Targeting with Segments in SAP Feature Flags
entry.content:
Managing feature releases becomes significantly easier when you can organize users into meaningful groups. That's where segments come in, they are reusable lists that let you target specific user groups consistently across multiple feature flags in the same service instance.

What Makes Segments Useful?

Think of segments as smart user lists you create once and reuse everywhere within your Feature Flags service instance. Instead of manually defining the same user group for each feature flag, you can reference a single segment across your entire service instance.

Each segment can include users in two ways:

  • Direct deliveries: Add up to 1,000 specific user IDs.
  • Rule-based targeting: Create up to 15 conditional rules based on user attributes like region or account type.

For more information, see Concepts - Segments.

Creating Your First Segment

Getting started is straightforward:

  1. Open the Feature Flags Dashboard and select your service instance.
  2. Navigate to Segments and choose Create Segment.Create Segment form
  3. Choose a unique name (starting with a letter, using only: lowercase letters (a-z), uppercase letters (A-Z), underscores (_), and digits (0-9)).
  4. Add users either by listing specific IDs or by defining attribute-based rules.
  5. Create your segment.

Your segment is now ready to use across any feature flag in that service instance.

A Few Things to Remember

You can create up to 20 segments per service instance. Keep in mind that segments currently in use can't be deleted, the system will show you where they're referenced if you try. This safeguard prevents accidental disruptions to your active feature flags.

Segments give you the flexibility to manage user targeting at scale while keeping your feature flag configurations clean and maintainable.

entry.title: Agent-Driven Development: Why Software Engineering Needs a New Mental Model
entry.content:
Forget everything you thought you knew about building software. The world of AI agents is exploding, and with it, a fundamental shift in how we develop, debug, and understand our applications. If you're still treating your agents like traditional software, you're already behind!

Let's dive into why your agent development mindset needs a serious upgrade.

 

The Old Way: Predictable Code
Think about a standard app. If you process a refund, you expect a clear, defined sequence:

  • Card refunded.
  • Ledger updated.
  • User notified.

Every step is hardcoded. If something breaks, you pull up the logs, pinpoint the exact line of code, and fix it. Simple, right? You're in control, the code is the logic.

The Agent Way: Intelligent Decisions (and Mystery!)
Now, imagine an AI agent handling that refund. You've given it tools (like a "refund tool" or a "notify user tool") and a goal. But here's the twist:

  • The same input can lead to different reasoning paths.
  • The agent decides which tools to call and when.
  • The decisions aren't yours anymore, they're the agent's.

Your code becomes the scaffolding -- defining the model, the tools, and the prompt. But the actual "brain" making choices is the AI itself. So, when things go wrong (and they will!), where do you even begin to look?

Why "Debugging" Agents is a Dead End
You're scanning logs, wondering if it "hallucinated" or if the "context window overflowed." The problem? You're trying to debug a dynamic, intelligent system with tools designed for static, predictable code. It's like trying to fix a self-driving car by looking at the engine manual, you need to understand its thought process!

This is where the mind-shift begins.

Enter the Trace: Your Agent's "Thought Diary"
Since we can't see inside an AI model's head, we observe its actions. Every prompt it sends, every tool it calls, every step it takes, and every message it generates leaves a measurable signal.

These signals, combined, reconstruct the complete sequence of actions an agent takes for a single run. This is called a Trace.

What a Trace captures:

  • The Model's Reasoning: Why did it choose to do what it did?
  • Tool Calls: Which tools were activated, and with what parameters?
  • Outputs: What was the result of each step?
  • Timing & Cost: How long did each step take, and how much did it cost?

Imagine your agent is trying to book a flight. A trace shows you:
"User asked for flights to Paris. Agent decided to use FlightSearchTool with parameters {destination: Paris, date: tomorrow}. FlightSearchTool returned 3 options. Agent then decided to ask user for preferred time."

This is gold! It's your agent's entire thought process laid bare.

Beyond a Single Run: Threads for Conversations
Agents often have complex, multi-turn interactions. When a user chats with your agent, each message generates a new trace. These individual traces are grouped into a Thread, representing the full conversation history. Threads let you see how your agent's behavior evolves across multiple turns, learning and adapting (or failing!) over time.

The big takeaway: When your agent misbehaves, the answer isn't in your Python file, it's in the trace, or it's in the thread!

The New Playbook: Agent Engineering Reimagined
So, how does this "trace-first" approach change everything?

  1. Debugging is now Trace Analysis:
    • Old: "Where's the bug in my code?"
    • New: "Let's examine the trace to understand why the agent made that decision."
    • Example: Your customer support agent gives a wrong answer. Instead of reviewing your Python functions, you pull up the trace and see it incorrectly extracted the customer's ID from a long message before calling the lookupCustomer tool. Aha!
  2. Evals Replace Unit Tests:
    • Old: assert output == "expected string"
    • New: Your agent's logic lives in its traces. You need to test these traces with evaluations (evals).
    • Example: You update your prompt. To ensure it still answers common questions correctly, you run "evals" on a dataset of past traces. An eval might check if the agent correctly used the productRecommendation tool and provided a helpful response, even if the wording changes. You can run these on past data or monitor them live.
  3. Product Analytics Becomes Trace Analytics:
    • Old: Track clicks, page views, and conversion funnels.
    • New: The same traces you use to debug also reveal rich insights into user behavior, friction points, and failure modes.
    • Example: You notice a pattern in your traces: users repeatedly ask for "pricing" but the agent always tries to book a demo first. The trace shows the agent misinterpreted the user's initial intent. This isn't just a bug, it's a product insight showing a user journey friction.

Observability: From "Exhaust" to "Fuel"
In traditional software, observability (logs, metrics) is often seen as "exhaust", passive data you monitor. In the agent world, observability is fuel. Traces power every single workflow that improves your agent: debugging, testing, and understanding user behavior. Your observability platform isn't just for incident response, it's where your entire team collaborates to refine your intelligent agents.

Ready to Shift Your Mindset?
Next time your agent behaves unexpectedly, don't ask to see the logs, ask to see the trace. It's the key to truly understanding, iterating on, and mastering your AI agents.

Hi Peter,
1. You can do cross company allocations in both S/4HANA Private and public cloud editions. 
In private cloud, the universal allocation supports Intercompany allocations since 2021, so if you are in an earlier release, you would need to use the classic GUI cost allocations (like KSU1/3/5).

entry.title: Re: Exporting ALV Reports to Excel in S/4HANA
entry.content:
In S/4HANA, Excel export only includes fields that are part of the ALV field catalog and not marked as technical or non-exportable. Even if a field is visible on screen, it will not be exported if NO_OUT, TECH, or NO_EXCEL is set, or if the field is added only via layout or hidden in CDS annotations. Ensure all required fields are in the field catalog and explicitly exportable.

 

 

ls_fcat-no_out = abap_false.
ls_fcat-tech = abap_false.
ls_fcat-no_excel = abap_false.

entry.title: Re: Exporting ALV Reports to Excel in S/4HANA
entry.content:
ls_fcat-no_out = abap_false.
ls_fcat-tech = abap_false.
ls_fcat-no_excel = abap_false.

entry.title: Re: Exporting ALV Reports to Excel in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Good point. This system is on SAP S/4HANA 2023, and we’re aware that ALV was significantly rewritten in this release. There are indeed several known issues in FPS 00 and FPS 01, especially around export to spreadsheet, which are documented under component BC-SRV-ALV.

We are currently reviewing the relevant SAP Notes for this component and checking whether the issue is already addressed in a later FPS or note correction.

Regarding export: yes, the new ALV export functionality (supporting XLSX) is available when it is explicitly enabled by the application developer. In that case, the classic List → Export menu is no longer required. However, in our scenario, the standard ALV export seems to be restricted, so we still need to verify whether the application exposes the new ALV export APIs.

Thanks for pointing this out — we’ll definitely validate both the FPS level and the developer-enabled export option.

entry.title: Re: Exporting ALV Reports to Excel in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @ronaldo_aparecido,

Yes, you’re right — there are limitations with ALV export to XLS/XLSX, especially in S/4HANA 2023 after the ALV rewrite. That’s exactly what we’re seeing here.

The report is a custom ALV report (not a standard SAP one), and the export is done via the standard ALV Excel export. On ECC 6.0, the same report exported all visible fields correctly, but in S/4HANA only a subset of the columns is included in the spreadsheet.

At the moment, it looks like this is related to:

  • The new ALV export framework

  • Field catalog restrictions (technical / non-exportable fields)

  • And possibly known issues in FPS 00 / FPS 01 under BC-SRV-ALV

We’re reviewing SAP Notes and also checking whether switching to the new ALV XLSX export (developer-enabled) is feasible in this case.

entry.title: Re: Availability of AIF Fiori Apps in S/4HANA Public vs P...
entry.content:
Hello John,

The SAP Fiori apps for SAP Application Interface Framework (AIF)— such as those for Message Monitoring(e.g., Overview Message Monitoring, Message Monitoring for Administrators/Integration Experts), Assign Recipients, Message Dashboard, and related tools like Setup Alerting or Maintain Trace Level — are supported in both SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (also called Public Edition) and SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud (also called Private Edition), as well as on-premise deployments.

This is confirmed from the official SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library details for key AIF apps (e.g., Message Dashboard / Monitor apps and Assign Recipients), which explicitly list compatibility with:
- SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
- SAP S/4HANA (Private Cloud and On-Premise)

Support spans multiple releases, typically back to around 1610/1709 and forward to the latest (e.g., 2025 for both Private Cloud and On-Premise, with Public Cloud aligned to quarterly/annual feature updates like 2302, 2023, etc.). They require SAP S/4HANA as the backend product and are HANA DB exclusive.

Key points and potential differences/limitations :-
- In S/4HANA Public Cloud, AIF is a built-in, lighter/standard component (part of the cloud stack under SAP BASIS), with pre-delivered Fiori apps for message monitoring and error handling. No additional licensing or extensive setup is needed for core functionality, and it's often used for monitoring integration messages (e.g., APIs, interfaces). Blogs and help docs highlight Fiori UX enhancements specifically shipped for Public Cloud releases (e.g., new features in 2302 like alerting setup, trace levels, emergency corrections).
- In S/4HANA Private Cloud (and on-premise), AIF offers similar Fiori-based monitoring apps, but the full AIF framework may provide more extensibility/customization options (e.g., broader custom interfaces, additional monitoring depths) since Private Cloud allows more configuration flexibility than the strictly standardized Public Cloud.
- Core Fiori apps for monitoring/error handling are consistent across both, but Public Cloud versions emphasize simplified, business-user-focused capabilities with less admin overhead.
- No major exclusions or incompatibilities are noted for AIF Fiori apps between Public and Private Cloud in the sources; they appear uniformly supported where S/4HANA Cloud is in scope.

For the most up-to-date and detailed confirmation per specific app/release:
- Visit the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com) and search for "Application Interface Framework" or specific app IDs (e.g., F4515 for Overview Message Monitoring, F4516 for Message Monitoring). Check the "Compatibility" or "Availability" sections for your target release.
- Refer to SAP Help Portal documentation on AIF Fiori apps (e.g., https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/... for overviews).
- Community blogs (e.g., on SAP S/4HANA Cloud releases) and SAP Notes often detail cloud-specific deliveries.

If your system is on a recent release (e.g., 2023+ or 2025), the apps should be accessible via the Fiori Launchpad under Message Monitoring sections, with any differences mainly in extensibility rather than basic support. If you have a specific AIF app name or S/4HANA release in mind, I can help narrow it further.

Thanks & Regards

Preeti Kumari

When I enable streaming to true due to the large response payload from S/4, the S/4 OData version 2 API response paginates because there are too many records. The "next" URL points to S/4 instead of APIM. I found another Q&A post (Solved: SAP API Management - API Response Message Size and... - SAP Community) suggesting that with streaming enabled, the API policy can't modify the URL from S/4 to APIM. This is causing confusion for the API consumer program. Do you have any suggestions? Thank you.

Regards,

Nick

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / Mass Maintenance of Sales Are...
entry.content:
Hello @LukasKuhn 

Thank you for your question.

In Mass Maintenance, the system only permits you to change specific table. For KNVV_STXL, inserting action is not supported. So, you can only change existing rows but not to create a new Sales Area record by importing a file. This is why your upload got rejected. Below are answers to your questions.

Question: is there another action code that can achieve this? If not, what is another way to add Sales Texts to business partners in mass?

Answer: No. Since the table does not permit you to insert in the app, changing the action code will not help.

  • You can add Sales Area Texts in mass by maintaining the BP UI if volume is small
  • Alternatively, you can use the released Customer Sales Text API via the Business Partner OData API. This allows you to create text entries in public cloud, instead of trying to insert via Mass Maintenance. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/85043858ea0f9244e10000000a4450e5.html for more details.

Here is a quick guide:

Open Communications Arrangements,

Create a new arrangement for SAP_COM_0008,

Create or assign a Communication User and generate credentials.

Make sure you add your Hostname and save. Call the OData service API_BUSINESS_PARTNER and update the Customer Sales Area Text for the designated customer and sales area.

Question: My question is, is this table and field out of scope for the MDG processes? If so, is there an alternative way to maintain it in mass?

Answer: Yes, this is restricted in public cloud. So, ADRT is not part of the mass import/export scope in public cloud.

The public cloud provides the following options for Email remarks/notes:

  • You can use the BP communication data UI, if the note is for email. However, not ideal for mass creations.
  • You can maintain communication remarks through the Business Partner API.  This is the most scalable public cloud option. This is described in KBA 3403243 and Business Partner (A2X) – OData API.

To summarise:

  • Mass Maintenance cannot insert into KNVV_STXL. There is no action code workaround.
  • Use API_BUSINESS_PARTNER for mass creation of sales text and not MDG-style inserts.
  • Email notes (ADRT) not being exportable in Mass Maintenance is defined in public cloud’s scope.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Staging for Kanban Using Warehouse Tasks: Negative st...
entry.content:
Hi @Niklas_Anton ,
This is a known limitation of Kanban replenishment with control type 8 (Stock Transfer with Warehouse Task) in embedded EWM when nested HUs are involved. The system posts goods issue per sub-HU instead of at the main HU level, which can lead to negative stock. SAP has acknowledged this behavior in community discussions, and there are no standard corrections yet—only design recommendations

This is a limitation of embedded EWM Kanban staging with control type 8. No SAP Note fixes it yet; recommended workarounds are avoiding nested HUs, using delivery-based replenishment, or implementing a custom enhancement.

entry.title: Re: F1705 - Inbound delivery EWM cannot be created on ERP...
entry.content:
Hi @mauriciocastiblanco ,
As per your observation, and the behavior you’re describing is actually consistent with how EWM–ERP integration can be configured. It doesn’t necessarily point to a queue issue, but rather to the delivery replication trigger point in your setup.

This is not a queue problem but a process configuration issue. Your system is currently set up so that the ERP delivery is only created after unloading/packing in EWM. If you want the ERP delivery to appear immediately after inbound delivery creation in EWM, you’ll need to adjust the delivery replication process profile/customizing so that replication occurs at creation rather than after warehouse activities.

entry.title: Re: Impact of Time-Dependent Service Cost Level on Progre...
entry.content:
Hi Minsung_Lee, 

In the Review Customer Projects app, the Percentage of Completion (PoC) is calculated based on the total planned cost (sum across all periods) of the ongoing plan version of the project.
It does not use the baseline version for PoC calculations.

This means that when time‑dependent service cost levels vary across periods, the system relies on the planned costs from the currently active (ongoing) plan version, not the original baseline. As a result, the planned‑cost denominator used for PoC reflects the updated period‑specific rates included in the ongoing plan.

Hope this helps in understanding.

Kind Regards

Harish Gour

entry.title: Re: Purchase Order Accruals – Configuration in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi together,

has anybody experience or knows how to configure or influence the posting date of the period posting run for e.g. the PO accruals/deferrals ?
By default the posting date is always the last day of the current period, but our business would like to have e.g. the deferrals posted already on the 1st day of the period.

The reason for this is that the costs that were already posted with the prepaid invoice can then already be checked even at the start of the month. In case of missing amounts and booking errors, they will have a full month to check and request adjustments.
Currently, the business colleagues must wait till the end of the month to make sure all postings are done as requested which leaves very little time to make changes in case of errors during the monthly closing process.

Best Regards,
Günter HUMPF

entry.title: Re: In cycle counting, how do we add found material?
entry.content: Best practice in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud is to formally record any “found” material through the physical inventory process, not just write it in. You should create a handling unit or assign the material to a storage bin, then post the inventory adjustment so finance sees a controlled, auditable entry. This avoids uncontrolled postings that frustrate finance and ensures compliance with audit requirements.

entry.title: Re: Fetch data in Adobe forms using standard cds in SAP P...
entry.content:
Good day @Rakshna 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, there are alternatives. However, it also comes with some limitations.

Does your data exist in standard released form data sources? If the answer to that question is yes, then use Data Source Extension with standard fields. Bind those fields in the custom form template.

See the  following references for more details https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/579920be820f45969e061197aca34484.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/fc1eb1eddef8476b8a6438256e73e7bc.html

If the answer is no, then the public cloud only supports adding custom fields that is filled via a released BAdI/custom logic and then expose in form data source. This is just your normal standard extensibility pattern forms offers. See https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/aff0b3f5f46c42a2b2c0fabfc233bab2/65f5fd6ac102431fab0974c19c24f220.html for more insights.

Here is a quick check guide:

In your Maintain Form Templates app, check whether the fields you require are already available.

If you don’t find it, then create a Data Source Extension in the Custom Logic app and include available standard fields first. Save the creation and publish. Open the form template extension and bind the new fields you exposed.

Let me know if you have further questions.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Is it possible in Public Cloud to generate the Qualit...
entry.content: Yes – in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud you can automatically trigger a quality notification when a defect is recorded during inspection lot results, but it requires proper configuration of defect recording and notification assignment. The system does not create notifications by default; you must set up inspection characteristics and notification types to link defects with notifications. Check SAP note 3599756

entry.title: Re: Sequence in which QA10 transaction (RQEVAI30) picks t...
entry.content:
Selection Logic: QA10 does not inherently process inspection lots in the order of creation. Instead, it processes lots based on:

  • The selection criteria you define in the variant (material, plant, inspection type, date ranges, etc.).
  • The internal sort order of the program, which typically uses inspection lot numbers but not strictly in ascending creation sequence.

Batch Job Behavior: When run in background, QA10 pulls all eligible lots and processes them. If multiple lots exist for the same material/batch, the program doesn’t guarantee chronological order — it may pick the latest lot first, then older ones, depending on how the database query returns results.

The safest approach is either:

  • Configure the variant to only pick the correct/latest lot, or
  • Handle duplicate batch lots manually to avoid conflicts.

entry.title: Re: Error while saving sales invoices - In comp.cd , the...
entry.content:
This is a tricky one, but the error message F5 153 “In comp.cd , the number range for 2026 has external numbers” is actually pointing to the accounting document number range setup, not the billing document number range.

To your question "Why it only affects some users, this is the confusing part. The difference usually comes from:

Transaction Variant / Customizing Differences
Some users may be using a transaction variant or a different billing type that points to a different number range object.

User Parameters (SU3)
Check if any user parameter (like XINNR) is set differently for those users, forcing external number entry.

Authorization Groups
Even if there’s no outright authorization error, some users may be routed to a different number range interval due to customizing in document type → number range assignment.

Fiscal Year Dependency
Number ranges are year-dependent. If 2026 is not properly maintained for all intervals, some users may hit the wrong one.

The error is not about authorizations but about number range assignment mismatch. It’s not really user-specific — it’s that some users trigger a different posting path (different billing type, different document type assignment). Check FBN1 for 2026, confirm the accounting document type used by billing is linked to an internal number range, and ensure no external range is accidentally assigned.

entry.title: Github Copilot VSCode - My Own SAP Development Rules
entry.content:
Lightweight Governance for SAP CAP and UI5 Projects

Creating clear, consistent project rules is one of the simplest ways to improve collaboration and quality in SAP CAP and UI5 projects. In this workspace, the guidance is organized into focused files: backend rules for CAP, frontend rules for UI5, Jest testing rules, and Bruno collection rules. Each file sets a crisp scope and avoids ambiguity, which is essential when multiple developers or tools contribute changes.

Why this structure works

- **CAP rules** enforce reusable types, managed data, soft-delete behavior, and strict service conventions.
- **UI5 rules** define naming standards, view constraints, and annotation boundaries, keeping frontend development aligned with backend services.
- **Testing rules** establish baseline quality by requiring meaningful Jest coverage for custom logic.
- **Bruno rules** standardize API collection formats, which keeps integration testing reliable and portable.

Together, these files form a lightweight governance layer. They improve onboarding, reduce review cycles, and keep the codebase predictable. For teams working across SAP CAP and SAP UI5, this approach offers a practical template for consistency without sacrificing speed.

Call to action

If your team ships SAP CAP and UI5 solutions, consider creating a similar ruleset and share your approach with the SAP Community. Small, well-scoped guidelines can make a big difference in day-to-day delivery.

Download, Use and Improve the rules:
https://github.com/radu103/github-copilot-vscode-sap-dev-instructions/
